LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, JOEL SHAMITOFF AND JOHN LUCERO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST CONTINENTAL FOR BAD FAITH REFUSAL TO DEFEND - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE DUTY TO DEFEND IS IMMEDIATE AND LASTS UNTIL THE LAWSUIT IS OVER OR THERE IS A SHOWING THAT THERE IS NO POTENTIAL FOR COVERAGE. SEE MONTROSE V. S.C. (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 287; CENTURY SURETY CO. V. POLISSO (2006) 139 CAL.APP.4TH 922.) DEFENDANT'S DENIAL LETTER IS BASED SOLELY ON 1 D (PRIOR LITIGATION EXCLUSION) STATING THE WILLIAMS AND RED CAP ACTIONS INVOLVED INTERRELATED WRONGFUL ACTS, THE FILING OF THE WILLIAMS COMPLAINT ON SEPTEMBER 1, 2008 PRIOR TO THE INCEPTION OF THE POLICY IS THE CONTROLLING DATE, AND THEREFORE THERE IS NO COVERAGE. HOWEVER, THE WILLIAMS AND THE RED CAP CASES DO NOT HAVE A TIGHT NEXUS. THEY INVOLVED DIFFERENT INVESTORS AND DIFFERENT REPRESENTATIONS MADE AT DIFFERENT TIMES. SEE FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT ADVISOR LLC V. AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL (2002) 506 F.3D 922. AT THE VERY LEAST, THERE WAS A FACTUAL DISPUTE REGARDING WHETHER THE WILLIAMS AND RED CAP ACTIONS WERE INTERRELATED THAT WOULD GIVE RISE TO A DUTY TO DEFEND UNDER MIRPAD, LLC V. CAL INS. GUARANTEE ASSOC. (2005) 132 CAL.APP.4TH 1058. THE COURT OVERRULES THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING. APPEARANCES - ATTY. KEVIN F. BARRETT OF GKC & J LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 352-6415 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CONTINENTAL CASUALTY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Once summary adjudication was granted in favor of plaintiffs, defendant should have realized that this motion was no longer tenable and taken it off calendar. A motion for summary judgment can only be successful if the moving party shows that it is entitled to judgment in its favor on all claims between the parties against whom summary judgment is sought. The grant of plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication necessarily precludes defendant from making such a showing. To the extent that the continued pursuit of this motion is a de facto request to reconsider Judge Woolard's order stated at the September 30 hearing, Judge Kahn, who is deciding this motion, is without authority to reconsider the September 30 ruling. Moreover, since defendant has sought only summary judgment, not summary adjudication, any argument directed to less than all of the claims must be rejected. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Plaintiffs Universal Interactive, Llc, Joel Shamitoff And John Lucero'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS UNIVERSAL INTERACTIVE, LLC, JOEL SHAMITOFF AND JOHN LUCERO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST CONTINENTAL FOR BAD FAITH REFUSAL TO DEFEND.

APPEARANCES: Jeffrey C. Burt, Esq. of Gilbert, Kelly, Crowley & Jennett LLP 213/615-7061 & Gordon D. McAuley, Esq. (appearing via CourtCall) of Gumbiner & Eskridge LLP 925/933-5800 for pltfs & moving parties; Andrew L. Margulis, Esq. of Ropers, Majeski, Kohn & Bentley 212/668-5927 for deft & opposing party Continental Casualty Company (appearing via CourtCall); Pamela L. Schultz, Esq. of Severson & Werson 398-3344 for deft American E & S Insurance Brokers California (appearing via CourtCall).

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE DUTY TO DEFEND IS IMMEDIATE AND LASTS UNTIL THE LAWSUIT IS OVER OR THERE IS A SHOWING THAT THERE IS NO POTENTIAL FOR COVERAGE. SEE MONTROSE V. S.C. (1993) 6 CAL.4TH 287; CENTURY SURETY CO. V. POLISSO (2006) 139 CAL.APP.4TH 922.) DEFENDANT'S DENIAL LETTER IS BASED SOLELY ON 1 d (PRIOR LITIGATION EXCLUSION) STATING THE WILLIAMS AND RED CAP ACTIONS INVOLVED INTERRELATED WRONGFUL ACTS, THE FILING OF THE WILLIAMS COMPLAINT ON SEPTEMBER 1, 2008 PRIOR TO THE INCEPTION OF THE POLICY IS THE CONTROLLING DATE, AND THEREFORE THERE IS NO COVERAGE. HOWEVER, THE WILLIAMS AND THE RED CAP CASES DO NOT HAVE A TIGHT NEXUS. THEY INVOLVED DIFFERENT INVESTORS AND DIFFERENT REPRESENTATIONS MADE AT DIFFERENT TIMES. SEE FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT ADVISOR LLC V. AMERICAN INTERNATIONAL (2002) 506 F.3D 922. AT THE VERY LEAST, THERE WAS A FACTUAL DISPUTE REGARDING WHETHER THE WILLIAMS AND RED CAP ACTIONS WERE INTERRELATED THAT WOULD GIVE RISE TO A DUTY TO DEFEND UNDER MIRPAD, LLC V. CAL INS. GUARANTEE ASSOC. (2005) 132 CAL.APP.4TH 1058. THE COURT OVERRULES THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order that tracks the tentative ruling. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
